title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Python Build Room
summary: A museum puzzle team incrementally builds keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface from original seeded fixtures while retaining earlier Python, graph, determinism, accessibility, testing, performance, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Provide a synchronized structured-text grid, route or step list, coordinates, and status that convey essential puzzle state.
Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.
Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Text Alternative
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop puzzle team handles case 7508 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Text Alternative.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-accessibility-evidence-1htbhz
# Competency: maze-accessibility-evidence.
# Case 6576: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_accessibility_evidence_1htbhz(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1htbhz = "1htbhz-zhbth1-6576"
    assert evidence_variant_1htbhz
    evidence_axes_1htbhz = (
        "grid1htbhz",
        "topology6576",
        "searchzhbth1",
        "event1htbhz6576",
        "access65761htbhz",
        "recoveryzhbth16576",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1htbhz)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"
